title: Notholaena muelleri
intro: Notholaena muelleri, also known as Mueller's scale fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. The Mueller's scale fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mueller's scale fern include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Mueller's scale fern is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Notholaena muelleri is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the fern family. This plant is a member of the genus Notholaena, which consists of various species of ferns. Notholaena muelleri is native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. The species is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and the division Pteridophyta. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the fern group.
